decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to control the game through disruption and removal while also generating advantage through cards like Preacher of the Schism and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Duress and Thoughtseize, while also having efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse. The deck also includes cards like Gifted Aetherborn and Mutavault for additional threats and ways to close out the game. The land base is designed to support the Orzhov color combination and provide consistency in casting spells.

The deck focuses on using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II as key combo pieces to generate advantage and create a powerful board presence. It utilizes various lands and support cards like Monument to Endurance and Thoughtseize to control the game. The inclusion of creatures like Iron-Shield Elf and Guardian of New Benalia along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse help to protect the combo pieces and disrupt opponent strategies. The deck also includes some utility lands like Geier Reach Sanitarium and Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire to further enhance its capabilities.

This deck focuses on setting up a powerful combo between Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate overwhelming board presence and out-value the opponent. The deck includes various cards to protect and support the combo, such as Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, Formidable Speaker, and Witherbloom Command. It also has disruption tools like Thoughtseize to control the game and ensure the combo can be executed smoothly. Overall, the goal is to assemble the pieces needed for the combo while keeping the opponent on the back foot to secure victory.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using low-cost creatures and spells. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, Burst Lightning, and Experimental Synthesizer, the deck aims to control the tempo of the game and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells and attacks. The inclusion of cards like Reckless Rage and Screaming Nemesis provides additional removal options and threats to further pressure the opponent. The deck's strategy revolves around maintaining a fast pace and finishing off the opponent before they can stabilize.

This deck focuses on utilizing cheap spells and card draw to quickly fill the graveyard with Arclight Phoenixes, which can then be brought back for aggressive attacks. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse to deal with threats, while Picklock Prankster provides additional card advantage. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure consistency and support the deck's game plan. The inclusion of Treasure Cruise and Opt further enhances the deck's ability to draw into key cards and maintain pressure on the opponent.

The deck focuses on slowing down and controlling the opponent's game plan through various means such as hand disruption, land destruction, and removal spells. It aims to establish dominance and maintain board control using a variety of spells and abilities while also utilizing the companion Yorion, Sky Nomad for added value through its blink effect. With a mix of disruption, card advantage, and removal, the Dimir Control deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver opponents in a more strategic and calculated manner.

The main concept of this deck is to use the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes support cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of cards like Fleeting Spirit and Seasoned Hallowblade add flexibility and resilience to the deck. The land base is optimized to support the combo and ensure smooth mana production. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently and quickly to secure victory.

This Red Deck Wins aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with low-cost, high-impact creatures and burn spells. The strategy is to maintain early pressure with cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while utilizing burn spells like Lightning Strike and Burst Lightning to remove blockers and finish off opponents. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional damage sources, while cards like Monstrous Rage and Reckless Rage help boost damage output. The deck aims to be fast, aggressive, and efficient in its pursuit of victory.

This Orzhov Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress, removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse, and creature threats such as Gifted Aetherborn and Preacher of the Schism. The deck also utilizes Mutavault and Brightclimb Pathway for additional utility and flexibility. The objective is to control the game by removing threats and disrupting the opponent's game plan before eventually closing out the game with powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse.

The primary focus of this Orzhov Control deck is to establish board control through removal spells like Vanishing Verse and Fatal Push, while also utilizing hand disruption effects like Thoughtseize and Duress to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Additionally, the deck aims to stabilize with powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Gifted Aetherborn. With a mix of resilient creatures and disruptive spells, this deck aims to outvalue its opponent and grind out a win through attrition and resource management.

The main concept of this Aggro - Arclight Phoenix deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with spells to bring back Arclight Phoenix multiple times. Cards like Consider, Sleight of Hand, and Opt help to draw into more spells, while Fiery Impulse and Lightning Axe provide removal for opponent's creatures. Picklock Prankster and Ledger Shredder help disrupt opponent's strategy, while Cori-Steel Cutter and Prof's Eidetic Memory provide additional value. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, powerful creature recursion and efficient spells.

This deck focuses on combining Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy that overwhelms the opponent. It utilizes cards like Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Raffine's Informant to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Esika's Chariot and Skysovereign, Consul Flagship provide additional value and threat on the battlefield. The deck aims to generate advantage through card advantage, powerful creatures, and strategic plays, ultimately leading to a dominant position and securing victory.

The focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards like Voice of Resurgence, Collected Company, and Formidable Speaker, the deck aims to quickly build a strong board presence and apply pressure on the opponent. Cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria help to slow down the opponent's game plan while Yorion, Sky Nomad offers additional card advantage and versatility. Overall, the deck excels at maintaining pressure while disrupting the opponent's strategy.

This deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures and powerful spells to overwhelm the opponent quickly. It includes cards for mana acceleration and fixing, along with a mix of creature and non-creature spells to maintain pressure on the opponent. The deck aims to control the board early on and establish a strong presence with cards like Omnath, Locus of Creation and Risen Reef. By combining efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Superior Spider-Man with powerful creatures like Ashling, Rekindled, the deck aims to win through consistent aggression and tempo advantage.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate a powerful advantage. It aims to control the board with removal spells like Thoughtseize and Bitter Triumph while ramping up with Cache Grab and Formidable Speaker. The deck also includes utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Takenuma, Abandoned Mire to enhance its combos. Overall, the deck seeks to overwhelm opponents with its powerful synergies and combo potential, making it a formidable force in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

The main concept of this Pioneer 1 vs 1 Aggro - Red Deck Wins deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using low-cost creatures and burn spells. With cards like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Kumano Faces Kakkazan, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and finish off the opponent with burst damage from cards like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional threats, while Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage help control the board and eliminate opposing creatures. Overall, the deck focuses on fast and efficient aggression to achieve victory.
